description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with GIT_SERVER and PROJECT_NAME variables and a global before_script that echoes "Starting CI Build of $PROJECT_NAME ($CI_BUILD_REPO branch $CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME)". Define a test stage containing lint, test_linux_64, test_osx_64, and test_win10_64 jobs; lint runs on the host-soft-ci-runner tag and executes echo "===== Running linters", python2 -m pycodestyle, and python2 -m pylint zaber. test_linux_64 runs on linux-64 and py3 tags, installs requirements with python2 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt and python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt, then echoes and runs pytest under both Python 2 and Python 3, while test_osx_64 repeats the same script on osx-64 and py3 tags. test_win10_64 runs on windows-64 and py3 tags, echoes "===== Running tests with Python 3", installs requirements with py -3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt, and runs py -3 -m pytest. Add a deploy stage with a publish job that runs only on the master branch when $PUBLISH_PYTHON_CORE_VERSION is set, is manual, runs on host-fwee-swci-ubuntu-x64-vm, and executes echo "===== PUBLISHING RELEASE", echo "Fetching publication tools...", cd .., conditional removal of a build_automation directory, git clone of $GIT_SERVER/software-internal/build_automation.git, cd into its publication folder, and runs python3 ./publish_python_core_library.py $CI_PROJECT_DIR $PUBLISH_PYTHON_CORE_VERSION --live.
